item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part of this is when they badgered the agent into inventing symptoms. (At least, that's how I decided to read it, heh.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The prospect of being trapped in a desk job hell terrifies me.
And I AM currently working a desk job.
+1

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I am 100% okay with this

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author approved edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions Agent LaFerrier, who appears in another tale. I propose linking to it here for that reason.

Editing this:

On September █, 20██, a test was conducted involving Agent LaFerrier.

To this:

On September █, 20██, a test was conducted involving Agent LaFerrier.
Holy fuck guys, holy gosh diddly darn. We made it to +100. Let us gather in joyous celebration!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short sweet and leaves just enough to the imagination without to many redactions. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The whole diversion was to make sure he wasn't an imposter, and he is now still under heavy supervision. It is healthy for the reader to wonder which agent is the real agent, or if both are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How have they not put the agent back in containment after the morse code transmissions? The guy who came back may be an imposter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The most worrying implication is that

+ Spoilers.
- Spoilers.

soon, his double is going to adapt to the situation and end up being another mindless worker at the office, in the world full of droning office workers in the world with black sky. And who knows what effect that'll have on his real counterpart.

- Spoilers.

Utterly fascinating!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a distinct flavour of classic Foundation, and I love it. Short and sweet achieved, and te concept is all the punchier for it.

I assume the black bars at the end obscure the agents first name? What must he be experiencing in there!

Enthusiastic +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, I love this. I can't help but feel bad for the agent trapped in the alternate reality. That second message about the sky never being blue sounded a lot different though. Is that the same agent?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I don't know if I said it before, but I was giggling while I was writing that line. I'm very proud of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally I was not buying this, but then the ending came and really tied it all together. The narrative here is simple, but it works.

Also, I agree with TL. "YELLING AT PEOPLE IN MORSE CODE IS NOT AS CATHARTIC AS YOUD EXPECT" is a great line.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*whistling*

…Might do that… with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt like the "is the Agent anomalous" bit was something of a diversion, but I love "YELLING AT PEOPLE IN MORSE CODE IS NOT AS CATHARTIC AS YOUD EXPECT". The story told by the guy's double is what makes this work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an attempt at retrieving the trapped researcher.

This actually sounds like an interesting tale premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're the author, you can re-write it whenever you want to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh,
Ytp has made me think of spongebob with similar fucked upness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hee, well, I really liked the name and decided to use it. I guess it is a spongebob reference anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe I'll rewrite it some day when I have more experience… But I'm not quite sure how that process works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one really piqued my curiosity, but while I get keeping it short and sweet, I think the concept of the Foundation "missing the curveball" falls a bit flat without some expansion on testing the anomaly (what happens if something flies towards the building? Can the inhabitants see the agents on the hill while they're observing?) or at least an attempt at retrieving the trapped researcher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Hillenburg,

I thought that we were saving that for a spongebob reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it enough for an upvote, my one gripe is that the dialogue in the interview seemed a little unnatural.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: liked it in chat, love it here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to see a little more in the way of the agents communications out to the Foundation, maybe talk about the other people there (or their personal effects if there is no one else physically there).Y'know, day 1, day 2 etc

This. This is exactly what I'd really like to see in this article. It's something that, done well; could take my vote from "+1 that was good", to "+1 Holy fuck that was amazing!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was trying to go for "short and sweet", so I didn't want to overcrowd it with every single small thing I could have put in, because there is a lot here. Such as:

  • Thing you just mentioned
  • What if they cordoned off that street corner in that pedestrian neighborhood?
  • What if they shot a sniper rifle into the building?
  • What if they tried to fly a drone into the building (starting from that vantage point)?

So there was a lot I could've done, but I didn't want the focus to be diluted. Thanks for the +1, at any rate, glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to see a little more in the way of the agents communications out to the Foundation, maybe talk about the other people there (or their personal effects if there is no one else physically there).Y'know, day 1, day 2 etc

Oh wtf have a +1 anyway

item.title: The Original Poster
item.content:encoded:
fanart except it's actually just me
i drew this <3

The idea of this article was to have a seemingly normal (or as normal as normal gets) anomaly that throws the Foundation a curveball, and the Foundation tries to hit it and fails. That's the idea. Tell me if I pulled it off well.

Of course, if you liked the article but didn't get that aspect of it, whatever. Your opinion is subjective and you can like and dislike what you want.

It took a while, but it broke +50!! Yaaay!

Wow, I never made a comment about how it broke +100. Hurray for that, I suppose. EDIT: Actually, I seem to have done that on its own separate post. Oh well.

Also, there is a tale about this now. I couldn't find a fluid way to link it in the article, but…
Hitting the Glass Ceiling EDIT: Due to stress, not negative ratings, this article has gone under.
